metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards (232 Total)
Let's go through the cards first — there are a lot of them, and they come in distinct colors.

The 42 green vine cards are your grape varieties. Each one shows what type of grapes it grows (red, white, or both), their values (1 through 4), and whether the vine requires a Trellis or Irrigation structure before you can plant it.

The 36 purple wine order cards are how you score points. Each one lists specific wines you need to deliver — fill the order with the right wines from your cellar and you earn VP.

The 38 yellow summer visitor cards and 38 blue winter visitor cards represent people who visit your vineyard. Each one gives a powerful one-time ability — summer visitors help during the summer season, winter visitors during winter.

You'll also find 18 field cards (3 per player, with values 5, 6, and 7 — these represent your grape-growing land), 24 Automa cards for solo play, and 36 mama and papa cards used during setup to give each player a unique starting position.

#### Workers & Markers
Each player gets 5 regular workers — small meeples in your chosen color. You also get 1 grande worker, which is a larger meeple. The grande worker is special — it can be placed on any action space even if it's completely full.

There's 1 gray temporary worker that goes to whoever picks wake-up row 7 each round.

Each player also gets a wake-up token (a little rooster), a VP token (shaped like a cork), and a residual payment token (a bottle).

#### Tokens, Coins, and Structures
You'll find 50 small glass-shaped grape and wine tokens for tracking values on your crush pad and cellar. There are 48 wooden structure tokens (8 types per player color) and 72 lira coins in denominations of 1, 2, and 5. There's also 1 first-player grape token.

#### Boards
The main game board shows the seasonal action spaces, the wake-up chart, and the VP track running around the edge. Each player gets a personal vineyard mat — this is where you'll plant vines, crush grapes, and age wines.

#### Action Space Scaling
The number of available action spaces scales with player count: 1 space per action at 2 players, 2 spaces at 3-4 players, and 3 spaces at 5-6 players. This keeps the competition for spaces tight at every count.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Getting Started
First, randomly choose a first player.

Deal each player 1 mama card and 1 papa card, face up. These give everyone a different starting position — no two players will begin the same way.

**Resolve mamas first.** Look at your mama card. Every mama gives you 2 regular workers plus a combination of card draws — vine cards, visitor cards, or wine order cards. Draw the cards shown on your mama and add them to your hand.

**Now resolve papas.** Your papa card gives you 1 grande worker plus lira, and often a choice — usually between getting a free structure or taking extra lira instead. Resolve these papa choices clockwise starting from the first player.

Each player takes their 3 field cards — the ones showing values 5, 6, and 7 — and places them field-side up on their vineyard mat. These are the three plots of land where you'll plant your vines.

Discard all mama and papa cards after setup — you won't need them again.

#### Optional Advanced Setup
For experienced players: deal 2 mamas and 2 papas to each player. Choose 1 of each. This adds more strategy to the starting position but takes a bit longer.

At this point, you should have 2-3 regular workers, 1 grande worker, some lira coins, a hand of cards from your mama, and your vineyard mat with 3 empty fields ready for planting.

subtopics.title: Year Structure
subtopics.content:
#### How a Year Works
Viticulture plays out over a series of years, and each year has the same 5 phases. Let's walk through them.

**1. Spring — Choose Your Wake-Up Time**
Everyone places their rooster token on the wake-up chart, choosing a row numbered 1 through 7. Lower numbers mean you act earlier in the turn order. Higher numbers give you better bonuses but you'll act later. This is one of the most important decisions each year.

**2. Summer — Place Workers on Summer Actions**
Starting with whoever woke up earliest, players take turns placing 1 worker at a time on summer action spaces. After placing a worker, you immediately resolve that action. When you can't or don't want to place more workers, you pass — and once you pass, you're locked out of summer for the rest of the year.

**3. Fall — Draw a Visitor Card**
Each player draws either 1 summer visitor card (yellow) or 1 winter visitor card (blue). Quick and simple, but the choice matters — pick based on what you need.

**4. Winter — Place Workers on Winter Actions**
Just like summer, but now you're using winter action spaces. Same rules: 1 worker at a time, take turns in wake-up order, pass when done.

**5. Year End — Reset and Age**
All grapes on your crush pad and wines in your cellar automatically age, gaining +1 value (up to a maximum of 9). Return all workers to your supply. Collect any residual payments you've earned. Rotate the first player.

#### Wake-Up Bonuses
Here's what each row gives you:
- Row 1: No bonus — but you act first
- Row 2: Draw 1 vine card
- Row 3: Draw 1 wine order card
- Row 4: Gain 1 lira or 1 VP
- Row 5: Draw 1 summer or winter visitor
- Row 6: Gain 1 VP
- Row 7: Gain the gray temporary worker for this year

#### How the Game Ends
If any player reaches 20 or more VP, you finish the current year (all remaining seasons play out), then the game ends. Multiple players can pass 20 VP in the same year.

subtopics.title: Actions & Worker Placement
subtopics.content:
#### Core Worker Rules
Here's how worker placement works in Viticulture.

You place exactly 1 worker per turn on an available action space. Once placed, that worker is used up for the rest of the year — a worker placed in summer cannot be used again in winter. When you pass in a season, you're locked out for the rest of that season.

Your grande worker is your secret weapon. It ignores ALL placement restrictions — you can place it on any action space even if every slot is full, and even if you already have your own regular worker on that same action. This means you can take the same action twice in one season. Save it for the moment you absolutely need it.

#### Summer Actions
These are available during the summer season:

**Draw vine card** — Take a vine card from the deck. The bonus slot gives +1 extra card.

**Plant vine** — Take a vine card from your hand and plant it on one of your three fields. Check whether it requires a Trellis or Irrigation structure first. The bonus slot lets you plant +1 additional vine.

**Give tour** — Earn 2 lira from giving visitors a tour of your vineyard. The bonus slot gives +1 extra lira.

**Build structure** — Pay the lira cost shown on the structure and build it on your vineyard mat. The bonus slot gives you +1 lira discount.

**Play summer visitor** — Play a yellow visitor card from your hand for its one-time effect. The bonus slot lets you play +1 additional visitor.

**Sell grapes / buy-sell field** — You can sell grapes directly for quick lira, or buy and sell fields. The bonus slot gives +1 VP. Important rule: you can only sell a field that has no vines planted on it. To sell a planted field, first uproot the vines — uprooting is free, just remove the vine card at any time on your turn.

**Gain 1 lira (cart)** — This small action has unlimited spaces. You can always take 1 lira.

#### Winter Actions
These are available during winter:

**Draw wine order** — Take a wine order card. The bonus slot gives +1 extra card.

**Harvest field** — Choose one of your planted fields and move grapes to your crush pad. The bonus slot lets you harvest +1 additional field.

**Make wine** — Combine grapes from your crush pad into wines in your cellar, making up to 2 wines. The bonus slot lets you make +1 additional wine.

**Fill wine order** — Discard wines from your cellar that match a wine order card to earn its VP reward. The bonus slot lets you fill +1 additional order.

**Train worker** — Pay 4 lira to gain 1 new worker from the supply. The bonus slot reduces the cost to 3 lira.

**Play winter visitor** — Play a blue visitor card for its one-time effect. The bonus slot lets you play +1 additional visitor.

**Gain 1 lira (cart)** — Unlimited spaces, just like summer.

subtopics.title: Vine, Grape & Wine Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Planting Vines
Vine cards show red and/or white grape types with values ranging from 1 to 4. You plant them on your fields — the ones marked 5, 6, or 7.

Here's the key rule: the total combined value of all vines planted on a single field cannot exceed that field's value. So a field worth 6 can hold a vine worth 4 plus a vine worth 2, but not two vines worth 4 each.

Some vines require a Trellis structure, some require Irrigation, and some require both. Check before you plant. You can stack multiple vines on the same field as long as the total stays within the field's value.

#### Harvesting
When you harvest a field, add up ALL the red grape values on that field's vine cards — place a single red grape token at that combined value on your crush pad. Do the same for white grapes.

For example, if a field has a vine with red 2 and another with red 3, you'd place one red grape token at value 5 on your crush pad.

The maximum grape or wine value is 9. And here's the great part: harvesting doesn't remove your vines. They stay planted, and you can harvest the same field again every single year.

#### Making Wine
Once you have grapes on your crush pad, you can combine them into wine:

**Red wine** is made from red grapes — the wine's value equals the grape value used.

**White wine** is made from white grapes — same thing, value equals grape value.

**Blush wine** combines 1 red grape + 1 white grape. The wine's value equals the sum of both grape values. You need a Medium Cellar to make blush wine.

**Sparkling wine** combines 2 red grapes + 1 white grape. The wine's value equals the sum of all three. You need a Large Cellar for sparkling wine.

#### Aging
At the end of every year, ALL grapes on your crush pad and ALL wines in your cellar automatically increase in value by 1, up to a maximum of 9. This is free and mandatory — no action required. Wine aging is one of the game's most important strategic levers. A wine worth 3 this year becomes a 4 next year without you lifting a finger.

#### Filling Orders
Wine order cards list specific wine types and minimum values. To fill an order, discard wines from your cellar that match what the card asks for and earn its VP reward. Your wines must meet or exceed the required values — you can use a wine worth 6 to fill a requirement for value 4, but you can't combine two lower-value wines to meet a single requirement.

subtopics.title: Structures
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Structures
There are 8 structures you can build on your vineyard mat. Each one is permanent once built, and you can only build 1 of each.

**Trellis (2 lira)** — Required before you can plant certain higher-value vine cards. Check your vine cards during setup — if any of them show a trellis requirement, this should be your first build.

**Irrigation (3 lira)** — Also required for certain vine plantings. Combined with the Trellis, this unlocks every vine in the deck.

**Yoke (2 lira)** — Lets you harvest or uproot 1 additional field each time you take that action. Great once you have 2 or more planted fields producing different grapes.

**Windmill (5 lira)** — Gain 1 VP each time you plant a vine. Only worth building if you plan to plant many vines throughout the game.

**Cottage (4 lira)** — Draw 1 extra card during the Fall visitor draw phase. This compounds over time — an extra visitor card every year adds up.

**Tasting Room (6 lira)** — Gain 1 VP each time you give a tour, as long as you have at least 1 wine in your cellar. Expensive to build, but powerful with a dedicated tour strategy.

**Medium Cellar (4 lira)** — Lets you store wines up to value 6 and enables making blush wine (1 red + 1 white grape). Without this, your wines are capped at value 3.

**Large Cellar (6 lira)** — Stores wines up to value 9 and enables sparkling wine (2 red + 1 white grapes). Includes all the benefits of the Medium Cellar, so you don't need both.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Ends
If any player reaches 20 or more VP at any point, you finish the current year — all remaining seasons play out normally. Then the game ends. Multiple players can cross 20 VP in the same final year.

#### All VP Is Tracked During the Game
There's no separate end-game scoring phase. Every VP you earn is immediately placed on the VP track as you earn it. Your score at the end of the last year is your final score.

#### Where VP Comes From
Wine orders are your primary source — each one is worth 2 to 6 VP. Beyond that, visitor card effects can award VP, wake-up bonuses in rows 4 and 6 give VP, and the Windmill and Tasting Room structures award VP when you plant vines or give tours.

#### Tiebreakers
If players are tied at game end: 1. Most lira remaining, then 2. Highest-value wine in your cellar, then 3. Highest-value grape on your crush pad.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities

**Build the pipeline first.** In years 1 and 2, your top priority is getting vines planted and the basic infrastructure in place. Draw vine cards, plant vines, and build a Trellis or Irrigation if your best vines require them. Without planted vines, nothing else in your winery works — you can't harvest, you can't make wine, and you can't fill orders.

**Protect your turn order when it matters.** Take an early wake-up row (1-3) when you absolutely need a contested action space this year. Take a later row (5-7) for the better bonuses only when timing doesn't matter. This trade-off between acting first versus getting bonuses is the heart of the wake-up decision.

**Save your Grande Worker for the choke point.** Hold your grande worker for the ONE blocked action you must have — usually Fill Wine Order or Make Wine in winter. If you waste your grande on something replaceable, you'll get locked out of the critical action later.

**Respect the field math.** Your three fields hold vines up to their printed values: 5, 6, and 7. Plan your vine planting to maximize grape production per field. A field worth 7 can hold a red 4 vine plus a white 3 vine for maximum output.

**Don't overbuild.** Only build structures your pipeline needs right now. A Large Cellar is useless if you haven't planted vines yet. Build Trellis first, then Irrigation if needed, then a cellar when you actually have grapes ready to become wine.

#### Common Mistakes
- Choosing a late wake-up row for the bonus while losing a critical action space to another player
- Burning all your workers in summer with none left for winter — a common mistake: workers placed in summer can't be reused in winter
- Using your Grande Worker on a replaceable action early, then getting blocked on Fill Wine Order or Make Wine later
- Selling grapes for quick lira instead of making wine for VP — grapes are worth far more as wine
- Ignoring aging — your grapes and wines gain +1 value for free every single year-end. Plan around this.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Viticulture

**The Setup**
Resolve your mama card first — she gives you 2 regular workers plus some starting cards (vine cards, visitor cards, or wine orders). Then resolve your papa card — he gives you 1 grande worker plus lira, and usually a choice between a free structure or extra lira.

Look at what you've got: 2-3 regular workers, 1 grande worker, some lira, and a hand of cards. Check your vine cards — do any of them require a Trellis or Irrigation? That tells you what to build first.

**Year 1: Spring**
Place your rooster on the wake-up chart. For your first year, Row 2 (draw a vine card) and Row 5 (draw a visitor card) are both solid, safe choices. Don't stress too much about this — just avoid Row 1 unless you desperately need to act first.

**Year 1: Summer**
Here's your priority list: If you have a vine you can plant (check for Trellis/Irrigation requirements), plant it. If your best vine needs a Trellis, build one first. If you can't do either, draw vine cards to improve your options.

Critical reminder: save 1-2 workers for winter! A common first-game mistake is spending everything in summer and having nothing left for the winter actions that actually score points.

**Year 1: Fall**
Draw a winter visitor card (blue). Winter visitors tend to have powerful economic effects that help you in the early game — things like gaining resources, training workers at a discount, or getting free structures.

**Year 1: Winter**
With your remaining workers, here's the ideal play: Harvest a planted field to move grapes to your crush pad, then Make Wine to turn those grapes into bottles in your cellar. If you can't harvest yet (no planted vines), use this time to Train a Worker (pay 4 lira) so you'll have more actions next year.

**Year End**
All your grapes and wines automatically age by +1 value. Workers return to your supply. Collect any residual payments.

**Years 2-3: Building the Engine**
Now you're running the full pipeline every year: Summer (plant vines, build structures) into Winter (harvest, make wine, fill orders). The first time you fill a wine order for VP, you'll feel the whole engine click into place.

**The Grande Moment**
At some point, the action you desperately need will be blocked by other players. That's when your Grande Worker saves the day. Save it for the critical moments — filling wine orders or making wine when every slot is taken.

**First Game Target**: Aim to fill 3-4 wine orders over the course of the game. A score of 18-22 VP is solid for a first game. Don't worry about winning — focus on understanding the seasonal rhythm and how each year your vineyard gets a little more productive.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Three Paths to Victory

**Path 1: Wine Order Engine**
Build a sustainable pipeline: plant high-value vines, harvest every year, make wine, fill orders. This is the most reliable strategy and what the game is designed around. Key structures: Trellis and Irrigation to unlock the best vines, then Large Cellar for sparkling wine on the biggest orders.

**Path 2: Visitor Card Engine**
Lean heavily on visitor cards for VP and resources. Some visitors award 2-4 VP directly with minimal cost. The key structure here is Cottage — that extra Fall draw every year compounds powerfully. This path has higher variance but can score fast when the right visitors show up.

**Path 3: Hybrid (Recommended)**
Run a basic wine pipeline for consistent VP while using visitors to accelerate. Fill wine orders as your baseline income and let visitor card spikes push you over 20. This is the strongest approach for most games.

#### Structure Priority
Here's the build order, roughly from most to least important:

1. **Trellis (2 lira)** — Unlocks the best vine cards. Almost always your first build.
2. **Irrigation (3 lira)** — Combined with Trellis, unlocks every vine in the deck.
3. **Medium Cellar (4 lira)** — Blush wine is the most efficient wine type in the game. A red 3 + white 2 = blush 5, often enough to fill mid-range orders.
4. **Large Cellar (6 lira)** — Sparkling wine for the highest-value orders. Build when you have grapes ready to use it.
5. **Cottage (4 lira)** — Extra visitor draws compound every year. Better the earlier you build it.
6. **Yoke (2 lira)** — Double harvests once you have 2+ planted fields producing different grape types.
7. **Windmill (5 lira)** — 1 VP per vine planted. Only worth it if you're planting many vines.
8. **Tasting Room (6 lira)** — Only with a dedicated tour strategy. Niche but powerful when committed.

#### Blush Wine: The Secret Weapon
Blush wine is often the most efficient path to filling orders because it combines two lower-value grapes into one higher-value wine. A red 3 grape + white 2 grape = blush wine worth 5. That's often enough to fill a 4 or 5-value requirement from modest grape production. Only requires Medium Cellar — much cheaper than rushing to Large Cellar for sparkling.

#### Wake-Up Mastery
- **Row 1**: Take this when you MUST go first for a contested action space. The cost is giving up a bonus.
- **Row 4**: Always take the VP, not the lira. 1 VP is almost always worth more than 1 lira.
- **Row 6**: Free VP with decent turn order. A great default when nothing else is urgent.
- **Row 7**: The extra temporary worker means an extra action this year. Take it when turn order doesn't matter and you need volume.

#### Worker Management
Train your 4th worker by Year 2 — more workers means more actions per year, which means a faster pipeline. Don't exceed 5 regular workers though — the returns diminish and the 4 lira cost adds up.

Split your workers between seasons: 2-3 in summer, 2-3 in winter. A common mistake: dumping all workers in one season and having nothing for the other.

#### Player Count Strategy
- **2 Players**: Only 1 space per action — blocking is brutal. Your Grande Worker is critical. Every wake-up decision is high-stakes.
- **3-4 Players**: The sweet spot. 2 spaces per action means manageable competition with room to maneuver.
- **5-6 Players**: 3 spaces per action, so you're rarely blocked. Visitor card strategies get stronger because longer games give more card draws.

#### Key Decision Points
1. **Year 1**: Build Trellis now or plant a basic vine that doesn't need one? Usually build the Trellis — it unlocks much better vines.
2. **Year 2-3**: Fill a small order early or wait for a bigger order with aged wine? Early VP is usually better — get on the board.
3. **Mid-game**: When should you upgrade your cellar? When you actually have grapes ready to use it, not before.
4. **Late game (15-18 VP)**: Every single action must point directly at VP. Play visitors for VP spikes, fill your best remaining orders, and don't waste actions on infrastructure you won't use before the game ends.

rules_citations.source: Official Stonemaier Games Rulebook — Viticulture Essential Edition

key_rulings.text: The grande worker may be placed on an action even if that action is completely full (it does not need the bonus slot). This includes spaces where you already have your own regular worker — the grande worker ignores ALL occupancy limits, allowing you to take the same action twice in one season.
Once a worker is placed in summer, it cannot be used again in winter that same year. Workers placed in either season are returned to your supply at year end.

key_rulings.topic: Game end trigger — finish the year
key_rulings.text: If any player reaches 20 or more victory points, the current year is completed (all remaining seasons are played). Then the game ends. Multiple players may exceed 20 VP.
When you harvest a field, you move grape tokens to your crush pad based on the planted vine values. The vines remain on the field and may be harvested again in future years.
At the end of each year, all grape tokens on the crush pad and all wine tokens in the cellar increase in value by 1, to a maximum of 9. This happens automatically and cannot be skipped.
Red and white wines can be stored in the basic cellar (values 1-3). Blush wine (1 red + 1 white grape) requires the Medium Cellar. Sparkling wine (2 red + 1 white grapes) requires the Large Cellar.
Each field has a value of 5, 6, or 7. The total combined value of all vine cards planted on a single field cannot exceed that field's value.
A player's victory point total cannot go below negative 5 (-5 VP). You may not play a visitor card or take an action that would reduce your VP below -5.
Once you pass in a season (summer or winter), you may not place any more workers during that season. You are locked out for the remainder of that season.

You may only sell a field that does not have a vine on it. To sell a planted field, first uproot the vines — uprooting is a free action (just remove the vine card from the field at any time on your turn). It does NOT require a worker or the Harvest action.

key_rulings.topic: Wine order filling — wines must meet or exceed
key_rulings.text: When filling a wine order, the wines you discard from your cellar must meet or exceed the values specified on the order card. You may use wines of higher value than required. You cannot substitute wine types or combine multiple lower-value wines to meet a single requirement.

key_rulings.topic: Grape values combine per color when harvesting
key_rulings.text: When you harvest a field, you add up ALL the red grape values on vine cards in that field into a single red grape token, and all the white grape values into a single white grape token. You do NOT get one grape token per vine card.

key_rulings.topic: Wine or grape devaluation when slot is occupied
key_rulings.text: If you would create a wine or grape token at a value where the slot is already occupied, the token devalues to the next lowest available slot. If no slot is available, the token is lost.

key_rulings.topic: Bonus action ordering — bonus before or after
key_rulings.text: When you place a worker on an action space that has a bonus, you may take the action and the bonus in any order you choose. For example, you can collect the 1 lira bonus from the Build bonus space first and use it toward the structure cost.

key_rulings.topic: Visitor cards — must be able to resolve mandatory effects
key_rulings.text: You may only play a visitor card if you are able to fully resolve all mandatory effects on the card. If a card says 'do X to gain Y,' you must be able to do X. If a card offers choices with 'OR,' you only need to fulfill one option.
